items.blurb: An 8-metre ribbon of AIUEO's sunshiny illustrations that turns any journal spread into a good-weather forecast.
Rub, press, reveal: watercolour blooms and a soft little rabbit transfer straight onto the page, no sticker edges in sight.
Tomomi Irago's woodland creatures wander under tiny stars along this Classiky tape - storybook margins for your notebook.
A quiet forest and a flutter of butterflies, printed 10 metres long. Classiky doing what Classiky does best: gentle magic.
Irago-illustrated critters trotting through the trees, one small strip at a time. Your parcels just got a personality.
A miniature forest that unspools for 10 metres - stick a treeline across your planner and call it landscaping.
Naoko Nakanishi's sketchy cats parade down this Classiky roll, ready to supervise every page of your journal.
Fifteen metres of loose-lined cats by Naoko Nakanishi. For people whose stationery, like their sofa, belongs to a cat.
Soft botanical bushes repeating down the roll - the easiest way to plant a hedge in your bullet journal.
Three pattern-happy Classiky rolls in one set - grids, dots and quiet charm for spreads that need structure with soul.
A crisp green grid, 10 metres long. Graph paper's prettier cousin, ready to label jars and tidy planner edges.
Clean blue grid tape that makes everything it touches look intentional - to-do lists included.
Shun Shun's breezy sketch of the Seto Inland Sea wraps around this roll - a coastline holiday for your letters.
Cloth artist mitsou lends her gingham sensibility to this blue grid roll - gift wrap that looks lovingly hand-sewn.
mitsou's red grid tape brings picnic-blanket energy to parcels, jars and journal borders alike.
Petal-thin paper stickers in spring shades - scatter them across a page and it genuinely looks like blossom fell there.
A pastel folder that gives your MU sticker hoard a proper home - seven candy colours, zero more crumpled sheets.
mizutama's limited-run cutter slices washi into skinny ribbons on demand - a tiny machine for maximum deco mischief.
A wandering cat pauses in the snowfall in this Tabi Neko postcard - winter in Japan, delivered in one soft scene.
Two travelling cats cross paths on a winter's day - a Toshinori Mori postcard made for sending to your own favourite companion.
Mori's roaming cat has a small autumn mishap among the leaves - the kind of postcard you frame instead of posting.
An inquisitive travelling cat makes a new acquaintance in autumn - one charming stop on Mori's seasonal journey through Japan.
Uni-ball One in 0.5mm: eight bold shades of gel ink that sit on top of the page instead of sinking in. Planner royalty.
The fine-tipped 0.38mm Uni-ball One - vivid, quick-drying ink for tiny handwriting and even tinier planner boxes.
Sketchy bears and botanicals in sunny yellow from Mind Wave - a limited run of instant good moods for your planner.
Soft beige bunnies and botanicals, loosely sketched and lovely - Mind Wave stickers that whisper rather than shout.
A whole sheet of peppy little bunnies bouncing through your to-do list - Mind Wave at its most morale-boosting.
eric's TIME KEEPER set stamps clocks, schedules and tiny time-things wherever your journal needs a deadline made adorable.
The IROHA set brings eric's eraser-stamp charm to photopolymer - hand-carved warmth, infinitely reusable.
Pretty Pattern hands you Igloo Dining's repeatable motifs on one tidy sheet - stamp your own washi-style borders.
July, stamped: sunshine-season icons from the 365 series to headline your midsummer spreads.
Nippon Natsu captures Japanese summer - festivals, fireworks and shaved-ice season - one clear stamp at a time.
A sheet of gentle greetings for cards and letters - because 'how are you?' deserves better than a text bubble.
March in stamp form: early-spring motifs from Igloo Dining to nudge your planner out of winter.
February's set brings the 365 series' cosiest little icons to the shortest month - stamp your way to spring.
Omamori charms and ema plaques in stamp form - press a little good luck onto every letter you send.
Wishing You is a full sheet of warm sentiments - the stamp set that writes your card's best line for you.
My Day turns daily logging into a tiny illustrated diary - meals, moods and moments, all stampable.
Autumn in Japan, distilled to a stamp sheet: momiji leaves, harvest treats and crisp-air charm for your spreads.
Amy Tangerine's brush-lettered phrases meet Sakuralala's crystal-clear stamps - instant hand-lettering, no practice required.
OH HELLO THERE opens every card with charm - friendly greetings drawn in Igloo Dining's easygoing line.
FAVORITE THINGS gathers eric's beloved small joys onto one stamp sheet - press your happy place onto paper.
AUTUMN HOME stamps eric's cosiest season indoors - warm drinks, soft light and stay-in energy for your journal.
NIPPON 2 continues the tour of Japan's loveliest little icons - stamp a whole trip without leaving your desk.
OLD ENGLISH pairs Igloo Dining's everyday-joy ethos with stately lettering - heritage type, homemade heart.
SCHOOL packs pencils, notebooks and classroom nostalgia onto one sheet - study-log decoration, sorted.
TABI means journey, and this set stamps one: trains, suitcases and wanderlust for travel journals everywhere.
The original NIPPON set: Japan's iconic sights and snacks rendered in Igloo Dining's cheerful line.
Fun Times is exactly that - a stamp sheet of small celebrations for pages that deserve confetti.
Let's Play brings games and giggles to the 365 series - stamp a little recess into your week.
Beautiful Days marks the good ones - gentle motifs from Igloo Dining for pages worth remembering.
Happy Days keeps a pocketful of cheerful icons on standby for whenever the week earns them.
Frame 2 is a sheet of stampable borders and boxes - draw-your-own layouts for planners with commitment issues.
Hikari means light, and this set glows accordingly - lamps, stars and soft radiance for evening journal pages.
FUYU wraps the 365 series in winter - mittens-and-snow motifs to stamp the season in before it melts.
EMOTION gives your journal a full range of feelings - tiny expressive faces to stamp beside every entry.
ACTIVITY stamps your hobbies in miniature - sport, craft and busy-day icons for habit trackers with flair.
FEELING BUBBLES puts speech and thought bubbles at your fingertips - let your planner talk back, politely.
A limited Fika edition of the Uni-ball One - coffee-break colours in a smooth 0.38mm line. Swedish mood, Japanese pen.
SARASA Grand puts moody vintage inks in a weighty metallic barrel - the gel pen that dresses for the occasion.
A correction-pen-shaped gadget that rolls out tiny checkboxes on demand - to-do lists have never been this satisfying.
Roll a ribbon of tiny meals across your food diary with one pen stroke - deco tape with a dinner reservation.
Miniature bento boxes, dispensed like correction tape - the fastest way to log lunch and make it look adorable.
One stroke plants a row of tiny garden motifs on the page - green thumbs optional, cute results guaranteed.
eric's complete wax-seal kit - handle, candle, spoon and moody blue-green wax - for letters that arrive like heirlooms.
Clear botanical tape with hot-stamped foil that catches the light as you turn the page - garden glamour by the metre.
Gelly Roll goes celestial: birthstone-inspired glitter inks from the brand that invented the gel pen. Sparkle responsibly.
A solid brass turtle concho from The Superior Labor that only gets handsomer with age - patina as a personality trait.
Pop out the pieces, tie the string, and a black cat swings forever after a star - a greeting card that becomes decor.
Chihiro Sonoda's artwork on proper Mino washi - one-stroke writing paper that makes even 'back in 5 mins' feel poetic.
Acrylic paper clips by eric that spin a full 360 - clip from any angle and let the artwork always face the right way up.
Big vinyl stickers born at eric's Japanese exhibitions - her favourite small things, laptop-sized, and going fast.
Tobimatsu's cats chase foil-stamped stars down 7 shimmering metres - a night sky you can stick to anything.
Hot-stamped frames from Shoichiro Tobimatsu that glint as they border your pages - gilding, but make it washi.
Nine foil-kissed designs of cats on a star hunt, ready to scatter sparkle across envelopes and journal pages.
A pack of Tobimatsu's shimmering frame stickers - nine little windows to spotlight whatever your page is proudest of.
Fifty sheets of swans and sweets across five designs - to-do lists with the elegance of a patisserie window.
Tobimatsu's bears run a very serious bakery on this sticker sheet - bread, buns and bear-sized dedication.
The Bear's Stationery Club is in session: studious bears with pens and paper, for stickering stationery about stationery.
A bakery-themed pochibukuro for gifting cash, stamps or secret flake stickers - tiny envelope, big charm, one dollar.
